user_query:
I’m preparing a brief for an upcoming planning session on urban convoy security and need your help to ground it in solid historical context. Please do the following and keep all outputs under the workspace directory.

1) Download two or more official, publicly available PDF documents relevant to convoy security, convoy operations, or urban movement protection from one or more of these official domains only: history.army.mil (U.S. Army Center of Military History), govinfo.gov (U.S. Government Publishing Office), nato.int (NATO official publications), or marines.mil (U.S. Marine Corps doctrine/library). Do not use non-official or paywalled sources. Save the PDFs under workspace/downloads/ using safe filenames.

2) Create a structured metadata file at workspace/output/sources.json containing an array of objects (one per downloaded document) with these fields for each: 
- title (string)
- issuing_organization (string)
- publication_year (number or string)
- publication_id (string or null if not stated)
- doc_type (e.g., "field manual", "doctrine publication", "monograph", "report")
- source_url (the exact URL of the downloaded PDF)
- domain (e.g., "history.army.mil")
- local_path (relative path to the saved PDF under workspace/downloads)
- abstract (1–2 sentences summarizing the document, drawn from the document’s executive summary or opening section)
- relevance_note (1–2 sentences on how this document informs urban convoy security)
All local_path files must exist, each domain must be one of the allowed domains above, and titles must be unique.

3) Draft a concise email to brief my commander, saving it to workspace/email/urban_convoy_context_email.txt. The email must include:
- A Subject line that starts with "Historical context:" and contains the phrase "urban convoy security".
- A greeting to COL Dana Reeves.
- A 2–3 sentence overview explaining the purpose (to inform our upcoming urban convoy security planning session).
- A "Key takeaways" section with 3–5 bullet points grounded in the downloaded sources.
- A "Historical references used" section that lists each source in the format: Title (Year) — Issuing Organization; Publication ID if available; and the local file path in parentheses. Use the exact title and year values from workspace/output/sources.json so this section matches the JSON.
- A brief closing that offers availability for follow-up or a live brief.

Deliverables to produce:
- At least two PDFs under workspace/downloads/ (each from an allowed domain).
- A structured JSON file at workspace/output/sources.json with the fields listed above.
- A drafted email saved at workspace/email/urban_convoy_context_email.txt that references the sources from the JSON.

Please ensure the Internet step is essential by sourcing the documents as specified, and keep the email professional and specific to urban convoy security. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
